e2e.notes: 14 tests from tests/e2e/portfolio.spec.ts (7 tests x 2 projects: desktop-chromium + mobile) + 6 pre-existing tests/visual/screenshot.spec.ts tests. Initial run required a rebuild of .next (missing vendor-chunks). Added data-fallback="core" attribute to components/SvgCoreFallback.tsx root <svg>.

recommendations.description: Largest Contentful Paint marks the time at which the largest text or image is painted. [Learn more about the Largest Contentful Paint metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/lighthouse-largest-contentful-paint/)
recommendations.title: Largest Contentful Paint

recommendations.description: A forced reflow occurs when JavaScript queries geometric properties (such as offsetWidth) after styles have been invalidated by a change to the DOM state. This can result in poor performance. Learn more about [forced reflows](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/rendering/avoid-large-complex-layouts-and-layout-thrashing#avoid-forced-synchronous-layouts) and possible mitigations.
recommendations.title: Forced reflow

recommendations.description: Polyfills and transforms enable older browsers to use new JavaScript features. However, many aren't necessary for modern browsers. Consider modifying your JavaScript build process to not transpile [Baseline](https://web.dev/articles/baseline-and-polyfills) features, unless you know you must support older browsers. [Learn why most sites can deploy ES6+ code without transpiling](https://philipwalton.com/articles/the-state-of-es5-on-the-web/)
recommendations.title: Legacy JavaScript

recommendations.description: [Avoid chaining critical requests](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/critical-request-chains) by reducing the length of chains, reducing the download size of resources, or deferring the download of unnecessary resources to improve page load.
recommendations.title: Network dependency tree

recommendations.description: Requests are blocking the page's initial render, which may delay LCP. [Deferring or inlining](https://web.dev/learn/performance/understanding-the-critical-path#render-blocking_resources) can move these network requests out of the critical path.
recommendations.title: Render blocking requests

recommendations.description: Time to Interactive is the amount of time it takes for the page to become fully interactive. [Learn more about the Time to Interactive metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/interactive/).
recommendations.title: Time to Interactive

recommendations.description: Resources are blocking the first paint of your page. Consider delivering critical JS/CSS inline and deferring all non-critical JS/styles. [Learn how to eliminate render-blocking resources](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/render-blocking-resources/).
recommendations.title: Eliminate render-blocking resources

recommendations.description: Reduce unused JavaScript and defer loading scripts until they are required to decrease bytes consumed by network activity. [Learn how to reduce unused JavaScript](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/unused-javascript/).
recommendations.title: Reduce unused JavaScript

recommendations.description: Polyfills and transforms enable legacy browsers to use new JavaScript features. However, many aren't necessary for modern browsers. Consider modifying your JavaScript build process to not transpile [Baseline](https://web.dev/baseline) features, unless you know you must support legacy browsers. [Learn why most sites can deploy ES6+ code without transpiling](https://philipwalton.com/articles/the-state-of-es5-on-the-web/)
recommendations.title: Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers

recommendations.description: Consider reducing the time spent parsing, compiling and executing JS. You may find delivering smaller JS payloads helps with this. [Learn how to minimize main-thread work](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/mainthread-work-breakdown/)
recommendations.title: Minimize main-thread work

recommendations.description: This is the largest contentful element painted within the viewport. [Learn more about the Largest Contentful Paint element](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/lighthouse-largest-contentful-paint/)
recommendations.title: Largest Contentful Paint element

recommendations.description: Source maps translate minified code to the original source code. This helps developers debug in production. In addition, Lighthouse is able to provide further insights. Consider deploying source maps to take advantage of these benefits. [Learn more about source maps](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/javascript/source-maps/).
recommendations.title: Missing source maps for large first-party JavaScript

recommendations.issue: Lines core→modules not readable in Projects screenshot despite opacity tween.
recommendations.patch: In components/three/ConnectionLines.tsx: bump default LineMaterial linewidth to 2.5, add transparent glow via additive blending + a thicker soft copy with 0.4 opacity; in ProjectsSection tween connections target to 1.0 (was 0.8); bump Bloom intensity or threshold to catch thin lines.

recommendations.issue: 'SYSTEM // BOOT' headline overlapping glowing core at low contrast.
recommendations.patch: Move hero headline off-center (grid-cols-2 layout on desktop) and raise to z-10 with solid dark gradient scrim (bg-gradient-to-r from-bg via-bg/80 to-transparent) behind text. Also boost tracking on display headline (tracking-tighter → tracking-normal+) and add text-shadow on hover.

recommendations.issue: Sub-panel skill lists are thin DOM; spec calls for ModuleBadge + SkillBar components showing per-module proficiency.
recommendations.patch: Create components/ui/SkillBar.tsx (animated GSAP-scrubbed width bar with glow) and ModuleBadge.tsx (icon+label chip). In SkillsSection render 8-10 SkillBars per active module, each tweening width 0% → skill.level% with stagger on sub-step activation. Flip between sets between sub-steps.

recommendations.issue: Projects scene state too similar to Skills; spec calls for an 'Execution Layer' feel with connection network activated and a horizontal pinned carousel of ProjectCard floats.
recommendations.patch: In ProjectsSection, in addition to opening connection lines, tween core to position [0, 0.6, 0] (slight lift) and scale 1.4 with slower rotation; change card layout from vertical grid to horizontal-scroll pinned row (ScrollTrigger horizontal pin tween x translate) — each card with rotate-y parallax; increase card z-depth with CSS perspective-1600 transform-style-preserve-3d.

recommendations.issue: Missing 'Ping the core.' heading, contact form / magnetic-button CTA, and proper '// 06' section eyebrow.
recommendations.patch: In ContactSection: add eyebrow '// 06 SIGNAL TRANSMISSION', headline 'Ping the core.', and a 3-field GlassPanel form (name, email, message) with MagneticButton (mouse-attract via GSAP quickTo). Keep existing CTA links below the form. Also strengthen the pulsing-orb effect with a clearer outward-ring keyframe (expanding torus or drei <Trail>).

issues: Hero headline 'SYSTEM // BOOT' overlaps the 3D core with insufficient contrast; no 'ALEX CHEN — DEVELOPER CONTROL CORE' primary headline or terminal Scramble lines visible per spec §9.1
ConnectionLines between core and modules are not visible on any section — either uDraw is staying at 0 or bloom/line-shader is not rendering the glowing lines (spec §6 + §7.3/§7.6)
Skills section missing <ModuleBadge> DOM cards and <SkillBar>s at section top; only 3D is present so skill labels (React, Node, Docker, AWS, React Native) are not communicated

Projects camera/3D state is nearly identical to skills — lacks distinct 'execution' treatment (spec §7.4: camera left to [-3.2,0.4,5.0], active module brighter, others dimmed). Horizontal ProjectCard carousel not anchored at section top

Contact section eyebrow reads 'SYS.SIGNAL // 04' but spec §9.6 mandates '// 06'; primary 'Ping the core.' heading and the contact form (Name/Email/Message + MagneticButton) are not visible in the 1440x900 top-of-section frame
Ground Grid plane (spec §6) not visible in any section — may be disabled or gridOpacity stuck at 0
PostFX bloom feels subtle — neon glow halos around modules are faint; would lift glass panels and line visibility if intensity ~0.9 per spec is actually reaching the render
On several sections (projects/experience/contact) overlay copy collides with 3D geometry reducing legibility; a right-side or bottom glass panel layout would keep text over solid glass

about.notes: Glass panel on left reads well: backdrop-blur + subtle border visible, eyebrow 'SYS.INIT // 02 — OPERATOR PROFILE' rendered in mono eyebrow, gradient heading 'Five years shipping production software.' Stats row (120+, 99.98%, 5) present inside panel. Camera offset right creates a distinct 'state' from hero. Core + ghosted modules linger behind — nice continuity. Minor: the right-side glass panel (if intended) looks like a dim empty rectangle overlay. Headline gradient stops slightly washed.

contact.notes: Dark bg + core pulsing with brighter cyan/blue emissive (core scale looks expanded 1.25 per §7.6 — good). Eyebrow shows 'SYS.SIGNAL // 04 - OUTBOUND' but spec §9.6 calls for '// 06' — numbering bug. 'Ping the core.' heading NOT visible — subtitle text 'Open to full-stack and 3D-web roles...' overlaps the purple module, hurting readability. No contact form fields (Name/Email/Message) or 'Send signal ↗' MagneticButton in frame. Footer text '© 2026 ALEX CHEN — DESIGNED & CODED IN NEXT.JS 14 + R3F + GSAP' anchors bottom. Palette and core state are on brand; DOM content is the weakness.

experience.notes: Eyebrow 'SYS.TIMELINE // 05 - HISTORY' and gradient headline 'A short history of shipping.' readable and distinct. A thin curved orbital/timeline arc path is visible behind modules (first hint of SVG draw/connection on any section). Core is slightly smaller (matches scale 1.1→0.75 per §7.5), rig tilt appears increased. TimelineNode list not visible in this top-of-section frame; DrawSVG path stroke animation not in-progress statically. Camera pulled back feel is consistent with spec.

hero.notes: Dark bg #0b0f19 is present. Cyan/blue core + 5 neon orbiting modules (green torus-knot, purple sphere, orange cube, blue shape, cyan bar) clearly visible — strong 3D presence. Gradient headline 'SYSTEM // BOOT' is readable but overlaps the central core, hurting legibility; needs a vignette/backdrop or offset layout. No visible 'ALEX CHEN' primary headline per spec §9.1. No terminal/Scramble lines visible in frame. Scroll hint tiny text near bottom. Grid ground plane not visible. Bloom/postFX appears mild.

projects.notes: Section eyebrow 'SYS.EXECUTION // 04 - SELECTED WORK', gradient headline 'Execution.' and sub 'Five production systems...' visible. 3D scene is essentially identical to skills camera — not distinctly 'execution' state. No ProjectCard horizontal carousel visible in frame, no active module highlight, no dimming of inactive modules per spec §9.4. Connection lines not visible (no bloom to reveal). ChromaticAberration spikes not evident. Overall this section on the static shot reads more like a reprise of skills; the horizontal pinned scroll content isn't showing at section-top anchor.

skills.notes: Clearest 3D frame: core icosahedron + 5 modules spread wide, neon palette holds (blue, cyan, green, orange, purple). Gradient headline 'Five modules. One runtime.' readable, eyebrow 'SYS.ACTIVATION // 03 - MODULES ONLINE' present. Missing per spec §9.3: <ModuleBadge> cards (should show 5 badges with skill labels) and <SkillBar>s — no DOM overlay of skills visible at this scroll position. Connection lines between core and modules not discernible. Distinct camera vs hero (modules spread wider, core slightly lower), state feels 'active'.
